amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: E. coli transports inorganic phosphate via two systems: Pst (high-affinity ABC transporter, Km ~0.4 μM) and Pit (low-affinity H+ symporter, Km ~25 μM). Under phosphate starvation, the Pst system is induced via PhoR/PhoB two-component system. PstS periplasmic binding protein captures Pi, delivers to PstCAB transporter. When Pst transports Pi, PhoU inhibits PhoR, turning off PHO regulon. Pit system operates constitutively. Phosphate regulation (PHO regulon) controls ~40 genes for scavenging and alternative phosphate sources.

name: Phosphate Transport via Pst and Pit Systems
notes: Shows AND gates (6) for ABC transporter complex assembly and ATP-dependent transport.
scientificAccuracy: Based on comprehensive characterization of E. coli phosphate transport and regulation.

sources.title: Structure of the PstB subunit of the Pst system reveals a critical role for PhoU in Pst-mediated phosphate sensing
sources.authors: Wang T, Nada S, Zhao M, Shi D, Ma M, Tan Y, Fan S, Cui M, Wang X
sources.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A.

sources.title: Global regulation by the PHO regulon in Escherichia coli
Structural basis for Pst system-mediated phosphate sensing and transport in Escherichia coli
sources.authors: Li Y, Guo C, Cao W, Zhou D, Cao W, Xu C, Lei M, Sun X, Li S, Liu P, Wang M, Han M, Tian C, Chen C, Guo R, Guo J, Shao F
